RESOURCE COLLECTION
Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health Epidemiology Unit
Data and reports about the health of pregnant people, children, and young adults in San Francisco.
Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health
SF.gov is the new home for SFDPH MCAH Epidemiology.
Resources
Data dashboards and data briefs
Health needs assessment data for women, children, and adolescents in San Francisco
Tables, graphs, and maps about women, ages 15 to 50, pregnant people, infants, children, and youths, up to age 24. 
Information about a wide range of health topics, including medical conditions, nutrition, dehydration, oral health, mental health, safety, access to health care, and basic needs, like housing. 
County-level prevalence rates, time trends, and disparities. Local information about national Healthy People 2030 priorities, such as preterm birth, infant death, childhood obesity, adolescent depression, and young adult substance use, high blood pressure, diabetes, and kidney disease.
Population data from the 2023-2024 San Francisco Maternal Child and Adolescent Health (MCAH) needs assessment to share with community partners, plan services, and promote health.
Number of live births in San Francisco
Total number of live births born to San Francisco residents by year
Child and young adult population by age and race
Changes in San Francisco's under-25 population from 2010 to 2020.
Preterm birth data
Data about preterm births in San Francisco. Numbers, rates, trends, disparities, and maps.

About
The Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health (MCAH) Section Epidemiology Unit of the San Francisco Department of Public Health regularly monitors population-level health data and health program information. We use these findings to identify issues that may affect families, pregnant and preconception individuals, children, and adolescents in San Francisco. Analyzing this information helps us improve health care and develop policies, plans, and services that ensure everyone in San Francisco has an equitable opportunity to achieve good health.
